summary: High‑energy first set with standout Jack Straw and soaring Morning Dew
blurb: The first set crackles with a chilling Jack Straw and tight transitions, while the second set recovers after a flat pre‑drums segment into strong jams like Wave to the Wind and a rousing Gloria encore. Hornsby’s piano shines on Dew and the band delivers a fiery send‑off vibe.
review: On March 23, 1992 at The Palace, the Dead opened with an electric first set that never let up. Jerry’s lead work on Jack Straw was described as “chills,” and his soaring solo on Morning Dew left listeners breathless. Bob matched his energy, especially during the Gloria encore where he laughed with Jerry and belted out a scream‑filled vocal. The second set stumbled briefly in the pre‑drums segment, but recovered through a tight Wave to the Wind, a seamless Crazy Fingers → Uncle John’s Band flow, and an inspired All Along The Watchtower that kept the momentum rolling. Bruce Hornsby provided beautiful piano textures on Dew, though some listeners felt his keyboards (and later Vince’s mix) leaned too far forward in the second‑set mix. Overall, despite a few rough spots, the show delivers high‑octane moments and a memorable send‑off vibe that makes it worth revisiting.

rating_calculation.sentiment_rationale: Multiple recordings label the show as positive, praising Jack Straw, Morning Dew, The Other One (monumental), and Gloria. Mixed comments note only minor flaws (pre‑drums flatness, keyboard mix) without any consistent negative criticism, yielding a solid positive sentiment adjustment.

best_recording.reason: Solid soundboard capture with clear instrument separation and few technical issues; includes all standout songs and the iconic Gloria encore.

key_highlights: Jack Straw guitar chills and emotive Morning Dew solo set a high bar early.
Second‑set flow shines with Wave to the Wind, Crazy Fingers > Uncle John’s Band, and All Along The Watchtower.
Gloria encore lifts the night despite a flat pre‑drums segment.

band_performance.Jerry: Delivers chilling lead work on Jack Straw and an emotive, soaring solo on Morning Dew; a few reviewers sensed early tension but overall stays locked in.
band_performance.Bob: Powerful vocal moments, especially on Gloria where he laughs with Jerry and 'screams his head off'; hits his stacks when Jerry leaves the stage.
band_performance.Mickey: Pre‑drums segment felt weak, but later drum jams received praise; overall solid foundation throughout the night.
band_performance.Billy: Same as Mickey – pre‑drums flatness noted, but strong contributions in second‑set improvisations.
